frameworks.name: leaky competing accumulator
frameworks.one_line: Neural network model of evidence accumulation for decisions and confidence

frameworks.name: tuned normalization
frameworks.one_line: Heterogeneous normalization tuning across accumulator units shapes confidence readout

frameworks.name: confidence as probability correct
frameworks.one_line: Dominant view that confidence tracks the probability the decision is right

frameworks.name: positive-evidence (response-congruent) account
frameworks.one_line: Confidence reflects evidence favoring the choice, insensitive to opposing evidence

core_question: Can a biologically plausible accumulator with tuned normalization explain dissociations between perceptual confidence and decision accuracy?
key_assumptions: Each accumulator neuron's normalization tuning sets its weight on decision vs confidence
Sensory neurons exhibit tuned normalization as a known property
Decision and confidence judgments draw on partly overlapping neural pools
manipulations: Varied tuned-normalization parameters across accumulator units in the model
Compared probability-correct vs positive-evidence readouts of confidence
Tested model prediction in macaque superior colliculus recordings
key_findings: Model reproduces several reported dissociations between confidence and decision accuracy
Confidence in the model is more sensitive to evidence favoring the chosen option
A model prediction was validated in macaque superior colliculus
interpretations: Seemingly suboptimal confidence behavior emerges from tuned-normalization circuit architecture
Confidence need not directly encode probability correct to match observed behavior
